mnemonics: VOW: ValueOf Wrapper - Use valueOf to get a Wrapper object from a String or primitive
keyPoints: Converts strings or primitives to their corresponding wrapper class objects (e.g., Integer.valueOf('123') returns an Integer object)
More memory efficient than constructors for wrapper classes because it may return cached objects
Handles number format exceptions when converting invalid strings
commonMistakes: Using valueOf(null) with primitive wrapper classes throws a NullPointerException
Forgetting that valueOf(String) can throw a NumberFormatException if the string isn't parsable
Assuming valueOf always creates a new object when it may return a cached instance
